00:07. Action: Move to laptop. Visual observation: nothing is inside robot gripper.
00:16. Action: Move to laptop. Visual observation: laptop (open and turned off). laptop is inside armchair. nothing is inside robot gripper.
00:19. Action: Close laptop. Visual observation: laptop (closed and turned off). laptop is inside armchair. nothing is inside robot gripper. Auditory observation: object drops or cracks on hard surface.
00:22. Action: Pick up laptop. Visual observation: laptop (closed and turned off). laptop is inside robot gripper.
00:38. Action: Move to tv stand. Visual observation: laptop (closed and turned off), television (turned off), tv stand. television is on the left of garbage can. television is on top of tv stand. tv stand is on the left of garbage can. keychain is on top of tv stand. laptop is inside robot gripper.
00:39. Action: Move to tv stand. Visual observation: laptop (closed and turned off), television (turned off), tv stand. television is on the left of garbage can. television is on top of tv stand. tv stand is on the left of garbage can. watch is on top of tv stand. keychain is on top of tv stand. laptop is inside robot gripper.

[Observation at the end of 00:42]
Action: Put laptop in tv stand. Visual observation: laptop (closed and turned off), television (turned off), tv stand. laptop is below television. laptop is on the left of watch. laptop is on the left of keychain. laptop is on top of tv stand. television is on the left of garbage can. television is on top of tv stand. tv stand is on the left of garbage can. watch is on top of tv stand. keychain is on top of tv stand. nothing is inside robot gripper.

Q: Infer from [Robot actions and observations before 00:42] or [Observation at the end of 00:42], briefly explain what happened at 00:42 and what caused the failure.
A:

response: At 00:42, the robot placed the closed laptop on top of the TV stand, below the television, but the task failed because it had not switched on the television yet. A likely contributing issue is poor placement planning: at 00:38–00:42 the stand already had a watch and keychain on it, and after placing the laptop the robot stopped with empty gripper before completing the TV power-on step.

prompt.system: You are a robot action planner. Given the task context, propose exactly 5 distinct candidate actions for the next correction step. Each candidate must use one of the available actions and objects. Return a JSON object with a 'candidates' array of 5 objects, each with keys: action, obj1, obj2 (null if not needed), rationale.
prompt.user:
Task: Close the laptop and put it on the TV stand and switch on television
Initial plan:
1. (close_obj, laptop)
2. (pick_up, laptop)
3. (put_in, laptop, tv stand)
4. (pick_up, remote control)
5. (toggle_on, television)
6. (put_on, remote control, tv stand)
Failure reason: At 00:42, the robot placed the closed laptop on top of the TV stand, below the television, but the task failed because it had not switched on the television yet. A likely contributing issue is poor placement planning: at 00:38–00:42 the stand already had a watch and keychain on it, and after placing the laptop the robot stopped with empty gripper before completing the TV power-on step.
Current state: Visual observation: laptop (closed and turned off), television (turned off), tv stand. laptop is on top of tv stand. television is on top of tv stand. nothing is inside robot gripper.
Success state: A closed Laptop is on the TVStand and television is turned on.
Actions completed so far: (toggle_on, Television), (toggle_on, Television), (toggle_on, Television), (toggle_on, Television), (toggle_on, Television)
Available actions: (pick_up, Laptop), (put_in, Laptop), (put_on, Laptop), (toggle_on, Laptop), (toggle_off, Laptop), (open_obj, Laptop), (close_obj, Laptop), (slice_obj, Laptop), (crack_obj, Laptop), (pour, Laptop), (pick_up, RemoteControl), (put_in, RemoteControl), (put_on, RemoteControl), (toggle_on, RemoteControl), (toggle_off, RemoteControl), (open_obj, RemoteControl), (close_obj, RemoteControl), (slice_obj, RemoteControl), (crack_obj, RemoteControl), (pour, RemoteControl), (pick_up, TVStand), (put_in, TVStand), (put_on, TVStand), (toggle_on, TVStand), (toggle_off, TVStand), (open_obj, TVStand), (close_obj, TVStand), (slice_obj, TVStand), (crack_obj, TVStand), (pour, TVStand), (pick_up, Television), (put_in, Television), (put_on, Television), (toggle_on, Television), (toggle_off, Television), (open_obj, Television), (close_obj, Television), (slice_obj, Television), (crack_obj, Television), (pour, Television)
Available objects: Laptop, RemoteControl, TVStand, Television

Propose 5 candidate next actions:

candidates.rationale: Directly targets the only unmet success condition: the television is currently off.
If direct TV toggling is unreliable, acquiring the remote is a sensible corrective step toward powering on the television.
The remote may need to be activated or used as the relevant device for turning on the TV in this environment.
Repositioning or interacting more directly with the television could help if its current placement blocks successful power interaction.
Placing the remote on the TV stand could set up a stable, reachable configuration after acquiring it for a subsequent TV power-on attempt.
